key_insight: The paper turns persistent aerial surveillance into a provably correct decentralized coordination problem by combining bounded LTL mission constraints with cyclic team reconfiguration and finite-time convergence guarantees.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical blueprint for swarm UAS operations that can maintain continuous coverage with limited energy and communication while preserving formal mission guarantees.
method: Split the swarm into stationary anchors and mobile workers with cyclic replacement modes.
Use a DNN-inspired local communication topology that is determined by the reference configuration, avoiding online graph optimization.
Optimize new worker reference positions by minimizing KL divergence between surveillance-node distribution and induced coverage density.
evidence_signal: Numerical simulations show cyclic reconfiguration, decentralized topology synthesis, finite-time formation convergence, and certified persistent surveillance coverage.
watch_item: The framework relies on accurate local models and a fixed mission specification, so real-world robustness to sensing errors, delays, or unexpected failures may require additional validation.
paper_title: Distributed Continuous Aerial Surveillance by UAS Swarms Under Formal Mission Specifications
